canonical_truth.summary: A severe storm system arrives with no warning interval — pressure drops first, then violent wind and rain force all ground activity to stop.
canonical_truth.location: wide-area forest; all branch locations affected
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: YY shelters in place across all branches; foraging and ranging impossible; eating happens during the wait; health cost from cold stillness

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: the sky changes pressure first — canopy bends wrong, then the full storm arrives with no warning interval

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: severe storm system sweeps multiple US states; tornado strikes Enid, Oklahoma with injuries; wide-area weather disruption forces shelter
authorial_note.translation_logic: a storm translates directly to physical environment — pressure drop, bending canopy, horizontal rain, forced shelter are all things YY's body registers; no abstraction needed
authorial_note.restraint_reason: storm arrives through sensory cues (pressure, canopy movement) before any announcement; the event is experienced rather than reported
